sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports intermittent dizziness, particularly when standing, and describes it as a feeling of lightheadedness. He denies any chest pain, shortness of breath, or palpitations. He also reports experiencing occasional, sharp pain in his jaw when chewing.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Blood pressure is 90/60 mmHg. Heart rate is 88 bpm, regular. Neurological exam is unremarkable, with no focal deficits.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient's symptoms are most consistent with orthostatic hypotension and possible trigeminal neuralgia.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: I recommend initiating a trial of gabapentin 300mg twice daily to help manage the dizziness and trigeminal neuralgia. I will also counsel the patient on the importance of slow positional changes and increased fluid intake. We will schedule a follow-up appointment in one week to assess the response to treatment.
